source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Analyses/Univariate/UnivariateAnalysis.cs @ fc28c0c -- A11 EXACT oracle, verified against the real RMC.BestFit UnivariateAnalysis by the dotnet gate. UnivariateAnalysis drives a Bayesian DEMCzs MCMC over a Normal model, then assembles the frequency (mode) curve + credible band on the exceedance grid. The point estimate + mode/mean/CI reproduce bit-identically across C++/R/Python (identical compiled core + bit-exact Mersenne Twister) AND against C# at thinning_interval=1 (the bayes_normal-proven path). thinning_interval is PINNED to 1: the SetDefaultSimulationOptions default (thinning_interval=20 for a 2-parameter DEMCzs run) exposes a C#-vs-C++ divergence in the THINNED population-sampler stream -- a real port bug documented in docs/upstream-csharp-issues.md (A11 Critical finding); the default-thinning path is a tracked follow-up. Short run (iterations 100, output_length 400). The exceedance grid ascends, so the mode (quantile) curve descends.
